content.problemStatement:
There are KKK travellers who want to travel through a country one after the other. The country is in the form of a 222-d grid and is divided into NNN rows and MMM columns, such that each cell of the grid can be considered to be a city.

Each city is initially marked with either 0 or 1. Let city (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) (the city at the intersection of the ithi^{th}ith row and jthj^{th}jth column) be marked with number CijC_{ij}Cij​. If a traveller is in the city (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), then


If Cij=0C_{ij} = 0Cij​=0 and j<Mj \lt Mj<M, then the traveller moves to city (i,j+1)(i,j+1)(i,j+1) and at the same time CijC_{ij}Cij​ is changed to 111.
If Cij=1C_{ij} = 1Cij​=1 and i<Ni \lt Ni<N, then the traveller moves to city (i+1,j)(i+1,j)(i+1,j) and at the same time CijC_{ij}Cij​ is changed to 000.
If the traveller cannot make a move from a city (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), then this city is considered to be the destination of that traveller. Note that the marking (CijC_{ij}Cij​) of the destination cell is unchanged — please see the third sample test for an explained example.

Each traveller starts their journey from city (1,1)(1,1)(1,1), and all travellers (apart from the first) start their journey only once the previous one has reached their destination. Find the destination city of the KKK-th traveller.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each testcase contains three space-separated integers N,MN, MN,M, and KKK — the number of rows, number of columns, and number of travellers, respectively.
The following NNN lines each contain a binary string of length MMM, such that jjj-th character of the iii-th string denotes the initial marking of city (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), CijC_{ij}Cij​.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line two space-separated integers xxx and yyy, where city (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) is the destination city of the KKK-th traveller.
constraints: Sum of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M over all tescases does not exceed 4⋅1054\cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The first (and only) traveller moves along the following path: (1,1)→(2,1)→(2,2)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 1) \rightarrow (2, 2)(1,1)→(2,1)→(2,2).
Test case 222: The traveller moves along the following path: (1,1)→(2,1)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 1)(1,1)→(2,1). Note that no move can be made from (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) by the first traveller.
Test case 333: The first traveller moves along the path (1,1)→(2,1)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 1)(1,1)→(2,1). After this, the state of city (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) changes to 000 while the states of the other cities remain unchanged.
The second traveller then moves along the path (1,1)→(1,2)→(2,2)(1, 1) \rightarrow (1, 2) \rightarrow (2, 2)(1,1)→(1,2)→(2,2).
